catalog_description: Event dispatcher patterns: declaring, broadcasting, binding across Blueprints, and handler wiring.
tags: event dispatcher delegate bind broadcast callback observer listener notify cross-blueprint publish subscribe

patterns.name: CrossBlueprint_DispatchAndBind
patterns.description: The publisher-subscriber pattern across Blueprints. The source Blueprint declares a dispatcher and broadcasts it when state changes. The listener Blueprint stores an object reference variable typed to the source, binds to the dispatcher in Construct or BeginPlay using that reference as Target, and handles the event with a Custom Event whose parameters match the dispatcher signature.
patterns.notes: Bind in Construct or BeginPlay, never Tick. The handler must be a Custom Event (not a function) because dispatchers are delegate-based. Handler parameter names must match dispatcher parameter names exactly. The bind node's delegate pin auto-wires to the nearest compatible Custom Event. In plan_json, use call_delegate (not call) to broadcast, bind_dispatcher to bind, and custom_event for the handler. Never call the __DelegateSignature function directly.

patterns.name: SameBlueprint_Dispatch
patterns.description: Declare and broadcast a dispatcher on the same Blueprint. Create the dispatcher first, then broadcast it from any exec chain. Broadcast passes parameter values to all bound listeners.
patterns.notes: The dispatcher must exist before any plan that broadcasts it runs. This is the simplest dispatcher use case -- no Target input needed since the dispatcher lives on self. The plan_json op is call_delegate (or alias call_dispatcher). Never use op:call with the dispatcher name.

patterns.name: SameBlueprint_Bind
patterns.description: Bind to a dispatcher on the same Blueprint, splitting handler logic into a separate entry point. Omit the Target input for self-context (self is implied). The handler is a Custom Event.
patterns.notes: This pattern is useful when the same Blueprint both dispatches and handles the event (e.g., a manager that reacts to its own state changes in a separate execution chain).

patterns.name: ComponentDelegate_vs_Dispatcher
patterns.description: Built-in component delegates (OnComponentBeginOverlap, OnComponentHit, OnComponentEndOverlap) are NOT event dispatchers. They are component-bound events that require specifying which component they belong to. User-created event dispatchers use a different mechanism (bind/broadcast). Do not confuse the two.
patterns.notes: Component delegates: specify the component name and target the delegate event name. User dispatchers: bind to the dispatcher name, optionally with a Target for cross-Blueprint binding. The distinction is important -- using the wrong mechanism will fail. Component delegates use op:event with component_name property. User dispatchers use call_delegate to broadcast and bind_dispatcher to bind. Calling a dispatcher's __DelegateSignature via op:call is always wrong.

patterns.name: Gameplay_To_UI_Dispatcher
patterns.description: The preferred pattern for gameplay actors updating UI widgets. The gameplay actor declares a dispatcher and broadcasts when state changes. The widget binds in Construct using a reference to the gameplay actor, and updates its visual elements (TextBlock, ProgressBar, etc.) in the Custom Event handler.
patterns.notes: The widget's Construct event is the correct bind point (not BeginPlay, which fires too early for widgets). Direct function calls from gameplay to widgets work but create tight coupling -- prefer dispatchers when multiple consumers might need the data or when the widget should be independently swappable.

patterns.name: DispatcherOpVocabulary
patterns.description: The correct plan_json ops for dispatchers: call_delegate to broadcast, bind_dispatcher to bind, custom_event for the handler. Never use op:call with a dispatcher name or its __DelegateSignature function.
patterns.notes: Common AI mistake: seeing OnFired__DelegateSignature in reflection and using op:call with it. That is the internal delegate function, not a callable UFUNCTION. Use call_delegate with target:'OnFired' instead. bind_dispatcher auto-wires its delegate pin to the nearest compatible custom_event.
